  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

Performance Tuning (tmc/perf.h)
[Tilera Multicore Components (TMC&tm;) Library]

Detailed Description

Access performance counters from user space.

Note: Before profiling, you should enable access to perf counters for user space by calling tmc_perf_enable_counters() or issuing "sysctl -w tile.userspace_perf_counters=1", or you can use /etc/sysctl.conf to set it permanently.

Data Structures

struct  tmc_perf_counters
 The counters are 32-bits, so for example, the TMC_PERF_ONE event counter will overflow in 3.5 seconds at 1.2 GHz. More...

Defines

#define TMC_PERF_NUM_COUNTERS   4
 Number of hardware performance counters.

Enumerations

enum  tmc_perf_events_e {
  TMC_PERF_INVALID, TMC_PERF_ZERO, TMC_PERF_ONE, TMC_PERF_PASS_WRITTEN,
  TMC_PERF_FAIL_WRITTEN, TMC_PERF_DONE_WRITTEN, TMC_PERF_SBOX_QUIESCED, TMC_PERF_CBOX_QUIESCED,
  TMC_PERF_TRACE_SAMPLE, TMC_PERF_CYCLE_12, TMC_PERF_CYCLE_8, TMC_PERF_CYCLE_4,
  TMC_PERF_ICOH, TMC_PERF_L1D_FILL_STALL, TMC_PERF_CBOX_FULL_STALL, TMC_PERF_LOAD_HIT_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_LOAD_STALL, TMC_PERF_ALU_SRC_STALL, TMC_PERF_IDN_SRC_STALL, TMC_PERF_UDN_SRC_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_MF_STALL, TMC_PERF_SLOW_SPR_STALL, TMC_PERF_NETWORK_DEST_STALL, TMC_PERF_INSTRUCTION_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_PFB_HIT_IN_PFB, TMC_PERF_PFB_HIT, TMC_PERF_CBOX_RESP, TMC_PERF_MEM_OP,
  TMC_PERF_CBOX_REQ, TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INTERRUPT, TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INT = TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INTERRUPT, TMC_PERF_INTERRUPT,
  TMC_PERF_INT = TMC_PERF_INTERRUPT, TMC_PERF_ICACHE_FILL_PEND, TMC_PERF_ICACHE_FILL, TMC_PERF_WAY_MISPREDICT,
  TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_PRED_CORRECT, TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_PRED_INCORRECT, TMC_PERF_VALID_WB, TMC_PERF_INSTRUCTION_BUNDLE,
  TMC_PERF_RESTART, TMC_PERF_USED_RAS, TMC_PERF_RAS_CORRECT, TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_NO_PREDICT,
  TMC_PERF_TLB, TMC_PERF_READ, TMC_PERF_WRITE, TMC_PERF_TLB_EXCEPTION,
  TMC_PERF_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_LD_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ST_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ATOMIC_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FLUSH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_INV_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FINV_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_MF_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_PFETCH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_WH64_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_DTLBPR_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FWB_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_LD_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ST_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_INCREMENT_READ, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_NON_INCREMENT_READ, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_WRITE, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_READ,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_WRITE, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_DATA_READ, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_INSTRUCTION_READ,
  TMC_PERF_REMOTE_DATA_READ, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_INSTRUCTION_READ, TMC_PERF_COHERENCE_INVALIDATION,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_INCREMENT_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_NON_INCREMENT_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_READ_MISS,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_DATA_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_INSTRUCTION_READ_MISS,
  TMC_PERF_REMOTE_DATA_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_INSTRUCTION_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_COHERENCE_INVALIDATION_HIT,
  TMC_PERF_CACHE_WRITEBACK, TMC_PERF_SDN_STARVED, TMC_PERF_RDN_STARVED, TMC_PERF_QDN_STARVED,
  TMC_PERF_SKF_STARVED, TMC_PERF_RTF_STARVED, TMC_PERF_IREQ_STARVED, TMC_PERF_ITLB_OLOC_CACHE_MISS,
  TMC_PERF_DTLB_OLOC_CACHE_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE_BUFFER_ALLOC, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE_BUFFER_ALLOC, TMC_PERF_ARB_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_MDF_WRITE, TMC_PERF_LDB_READ, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ST_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ATOMIC_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FLUSH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_INV_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FINV_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_MF_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_PFETCH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_WH64_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FWB_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ST_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NOFIL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NOFIL_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_RDN_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_QDN_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IO_READ_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IO_WRITE_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_I_STREAM_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_MDF_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IREQ_IV_VALID, TMC_PERF_UDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_UDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_UDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_UDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_IDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_IDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_IDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_IDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_RDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_RDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_RDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_RDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_SDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_SDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_SDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_SDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_QDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_QDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_QDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_QDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_UDN_DEMUX_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_IDN_DEMUX_STALL, TMC_PERF_WATCH, TMC_PERF_BCST0, TMC_PERF_BCST1,
  TMC_PERF_BCST2, TMC_PERF_BCST3, TMC_PERF_PCNT0, TMC_PERF_PCNT1,
  TMC_PERF_AUX_PCNT0, TMC_PERF_AUX_PCNT1
}
 Event definitions. More...

Functions

int tmc_perf_enable_counters (void)
 Enable accessing perf counters from user space.
int tmc_perf_disable_counters (void)
 Disable accessing perf counters from user space.
int tmc_perf_setup_counters (enum tmc_perf_events_e event1, enum tmc_perf_events_e event2, enum tmc_perf_events_e event3, enum tmc_perf_events_e event4)
 Select four events from the enum below to initialize the hardware counters.
void tmc_perf_read_counters (struct tmc_perf_counters *counters)
 Read the four hardware counters without clearing them.
void tmc_perf_clear_counters (void)
 Clear the four hardware counters.
void tmc_perf_add_counters (struct tmc_perf_counters *counters)
 Accumulate counts from the four hardware counters without clearing them.
uint64_t tmc_perf_get_cpu_speed (void)
 Get the CPU speed, in hertz.

Define Documentation

#define TMC_PERF_NUM_COUNTERS   4

Number of hardware performance counters.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum tmc_perf_events_e

Event definitions.

Enumerator:
TMC_PERF_INVALID  Invalid event code.
TMC_PERF_ZERO  Always zero - no event ever happens.
TMC_PERF_ONE  Always one - an event occurs every cycle.
TMC_PERF_PASS_WRITTEN  The event indicates that the PASS SPR was written.
TMC_PERF_FAIL_WRITTEN  The event indicates that the FAIL SPR was written.
TMC_PERF_DONE_WRITTEN  The event indicates that the DONE SPR was written.
TMC_PERF_SBOX_QUIESCED  An event occurs every cycle that the tile is in the instruction issue quiesced state.

TMC_PERF_CBOX_QUIESCED  An event occurs every cycle that a tile is in the coherence traffic quiesced state.

TMC_PERF_TRACE_SAMPLE  An event occurs every time a diagnostic trace sample is triggered.
TMC_PERF_CYCLE_12  The event occurs when bit 12 of the cycle_counter SPR is asserted.

The transition of this event can be used to generate periodic triggers every 4k cycles.

TMC_PERF_CYCLE_8  The event occurs when bit 8 of the cycle_counter SPR is asserted.

The transition of this event can be used to generate periodic triggers every 256 cycles.

TMC_PERF_CYCLE_4  The event occurs when bit 4 of the cycle_counter SPR is asserted.

The transition of this event can be used to generate periodic triggers every 16 cycles.

TMC_PERF_ICOH  The event occurs when an icoh instruction completes.
TMC_PERF_L1D_FILL_STALL  The event occurs when a memory operation stalls due to L1 DCache being busy doing a fill.

TMC_PERF_CBOX_FULL_STALL  The event occurs when a memory operation stalls due to Cbox queue being full.

TMC_PERF_LOAD_HIT_STALL  The event occurs when an instruction 2 cycles after a load stalls due to a source operand being the destination.

The L1 DCache hit latency is two cycles, so the instruction would stall on a miss but not on a hit.

TMC_PERF_LOAD_STALL  The event occurs when an instruction stalls due to a source operand being the destination of a load instruction.

This event happens on all cycles that stall except for the one 2 cycles after the load, which is counted by LOAD_HIT_STALL event.

TMC_PERF_ALU_SRC_STALL  The event occurs when an instruction stalls due to a source operand being the destination of an ALU instruction.

TMC_PERF_IDN_SRC_STALL  The event occurs when an instruction stalls due to IDN source register not available.

TMC_PERF_UDN_SRC_STALL  The event occurs when an instruction stalls due to UDN source register not available.

TMC_PERF_MF_STALL  The event during stalls for the Memory Fence instruction.
TMC_PERF_SLOW_SPR_STALL  The event occurs during stalls to slow SPR access.
TMC_PERF_NETWORK_DEST_STALL  The event occurs when a valid instruction in pipeline Decode stage stalls due to network destination full.

TMC_PERF_INSTRUCTION_STALL  The event occurs when a valid instruction in pipeline Decode stage stalls for any reason.

TMC_PERF_PFB_HIT_IN_PFB  The event occurs when an instruction lookup hits in prefetch buffer, already in PFB.

TMC_PERF_PFB_HIT  The event occurs when an instruction lookup hits in prefetch buffer, either in the PFB or in-flight.

TMC_PERF_CBOX_RESP  The event occurs when Cbox responds with fill data, either demand or prefetch.

TMC_PERF_MEM_OP  The event occurs when a memory operation is sent to Mbox.
TMC_PERF_CBOX_REQ  The event occurs when an instruction request is sent to Cbox, either demand or prefetch.

TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INTERRUPT  The event occurs when an ITLB_MISS interrupt is taken.
TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INT  The event occurs when an ITLB_MISS interrupt is taken.

(Note: internal name, deprecated. Use TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INTERRUPT instead.)

TMC_PERF_INTERRUPT  The event occurs when any interrupt is taken.
TMC_PERF_INT  The event occurs when any interrupt is taken.

(Note: internal name, deprecated. Use TMC_PERF_INTERRUPT instead.)

TMC_PERF_ICACHE_FILL_PEND  The event occurs each cycle and Icache fill is pending.
TMC_PERF_ICACHE_FILL  The event occurs each time a line in Icache is filled.
TMC_PERF_WAY_MISPREDICT  The event occurs when the Icache has to correct the way prediction due to a mispredict.

TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_PRED_CORRECT  The event occurs when a correctly predicted branch instruction is committed.

TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_PRED_INCORRECT  The event occurs when an icorrectly predicted branch instruction is committed.

TMC_PERF_VALID_WB  The event occurs when there is a valid instruction in pipeline WB stage (Note: internal name, deprecated.

Use TMC_PERF_INSTRUCTION_BUNDLE instead.)

TMC_PERF_INSTRUCTION_BUNDLE  The event occurs when an instruction is committed).
TMC_PERF_RESTART  The event occurs when the instruction pipeline is restarted.
TMC_PERF_USED_RAS  The event occurs when the Return Address Stack is used for jump instruction.

TMC_PERF_RAS_CORRECT  The event occurs when the value from Return Address Stack was correct.
TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_NO_PREDICT  The event occurs when a predicted taken branch that did not do a prediction due to bypassing branch predict pipeline is committed.

TMC_PERF_TLB  The event occurs when a data memory operation is issued and the data translation lookaside buffer, address into the physical address.

TMC_PERF_READ  The event occurs when a load is issued.
TMC_PERF_WRITE  The event occurs when a store is issued.
TMC_PERF_TLB_EXCEPTION  The event occurs when the address of a data stream memory operation causes a Data TLB Exception including TLB Misses and protection violations.

TMC_PERF_READ_MISS  The event occurs when a load is issued and data is not returned from the level 1 data cache.

TMC_PERF_WRITE_MISS  The event occurs when a store is issued and the 16-byte aligned block , at the level 1 data cache.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request.
TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_LD_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode LD.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ST_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode ST.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ATOMIC_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode ATOMIC.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FLUSH_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode FLUSH.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_INV_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode INV.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FINV_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode FINV.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_MF_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode MF.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_PFETCH_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode PFETCH.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_WH64_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode WH64.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_DTLBPR_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode DTLBPR.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FWB_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with the opcode FWB.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_LD_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with a non-temporal load.

TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ST_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID  The event occurs when the L1 data cache system processes a request with a non-temporal store.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_INCREMENT_READ  The event occurs when a read request is received from another tile off the SDN and the Level 3 cache will track the share state.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_NON_INCREMENT_READ  The event occurs when a read request is received from another tile off the SDN and the Level 3 cache will not track the share state.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_WRITE  The event occurs when a write request is received from another tile off the SDN.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_READ  The event occurs when a read request is received from an IO device off the SDN.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_WRITE  The event occurs when a write request is received from an IO device off the SDN.

TMC_PERF_LOCAL_DATA_READ  The event occurs when a data read request is received from the main processor and the Level 3 cache resides in the tile.

TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE  The event occurs when a write request is received from the main processor and the Level 3 cache resides in the tile.

TMC_PERF_LOCAL_INSTRUCTION_READ  The event occurs when an instruction read request is received from the main processor and the Level 3 cache resides in the tile.

TMC_PERF_REMOTE_DATA_READ  The event occurs when a data read request is received from the main processor and the Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE  The event occurs when a write request is received from the main processor and the Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_REMOTE_INSTRUCTION_READ  The event occurs when an instruction read request is received from the main processor and the Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_COHERENCE_INVALIDATION  The event occurs when a coherence invalidation is received from another tile off the QDN.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_INCREMENT_READ_MISS  The event occurs when a read request is received from another tile off the SDN and misses the Level 3 cache.

The level 3 cache will track the share state.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_NON_INCREMENT_READ_MISS  The event occurs when a read request is received from another tile off the SDN and misses the Level 3 cache.

The Level 3 cache will not track the share state.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_WRITE_MISS  The event occurs when a write request is received from another tile off the SDN and misses the Level 3 cache.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_READ_MISS  The event occurs when a read request is received from an IO device off the SDN and misses the Level 3 cache.

TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_WRITE_MISS  The event occurs when a write request is received from an IO device off the SDN and misses the Level 3 cache.

TMC_PERF_LOCAL_DATA_READ_MISS  The event occurs when a data read request is received from the main processor and misses the Level 3 cache resided in the tile.

TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE_MISS  The event occurs when a write request is received from the main processor and misses the Level 3 cache resided in the tile.

TMC_PERF_LOCAL_INSTRUCTION_READ_MISS  The event occurs when an instruction read request is received from the main processor and misses the Level 3 cache resided in the tile.

TMC_PERF_REMOTE_DATA_READ_MISS  The event occurs when a data read request is received from the main processor and misses the Level 2 cache.

The Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE_MISS  The event occurs when a write request is received from the main processor and misses the Level 2 cache.

The Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_REMOTE_INSTRUCTION_READ_MISS  The event occurs when an instruction read request is received from the main processor and misses the Level 2 cache.

The Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_COHERENCE_INVALIDATION_HIT  The event occurs when a coherence invalidation is received from another tile off the QDN and hits the level 2 cache.

TMC_PERF_CACHE_WRITEBACK  The event occurs when a cache writeback to main memory, including victim writes or explicit flushes, leaves the tile.

TMC_PERF_SDN_STARVED  The event occurs when a snoop is received and the controller enters the SDN starved condition.

TMC_PERF_RDN_STARVED  The event occurs when the controller enters the RDN starved condition.
TMC_PERF_QDN_STARVED  The event occurs when the controller enters the QDN starved condition.
TMC_PERF_SKF_STARVED  The event occurs when the controller enters the skid FIFO starved condition.

TMC_PERF_RTF_STARVED  The event occurs when the controller enters the re-try FIFO starved condition.

TMC_PERF_IREQ_STARVED  The event occurs when the controller enters the instruction stream starved condition.

TMC_PERF_ITLB_OLOC_CACHE_MISS  The event occurs when an instruction read request associated with the ITLB entry specified by ITLB_PERF is received from the main processor and misses the Level 2 cache.

The Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_DTLB_OLOC_CACHE_MISS  The event occurs when a data read or write, associated with the DTLB entry specified by DTLB_PERF is received from the main processor and misses the Level 2 cache.

The Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE_BUFFER_ALLOC  The event occurs when a write request is received from the main processor and allocates a write buffer in the Level 3 cache resided in the tile.

TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE_BUFFER_ALLOC  The event occurs when a write request is received from the main processor and allocates a write buffer in the Level 2 cache resided in the tile.

The Level 3 cache resides in another tile.

TMC_PERF_ARB_VALID  The event occurs when a request is processed in the L2 pipeline.
TMC_PERF_MDF_WRITE  The event occurs when a request generates a MDF write.
TMC_PERF_LDB_READ  The event occurs when a request generates a Load Buffer read.
TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with a load opcode.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ST_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with a store opcode.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ATOMIC_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode ATOMIC.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FLUSH_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode FLUSH.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_INV_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode INV.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FINV_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode FINV.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_MF_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode MF.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_PFETCH_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode PFETCH.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_WH64_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode WH64.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FWB_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode FWB.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode LD_NON_TEMPORAL.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ST_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode ST_NON_TEMPORAL.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NOFIL_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode LD_NOFIL.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NOFIL_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode LD_NOFIL_NON_TEMPORAL.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_RDN_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request from RDN network.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_QDN_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request from QDN network.

TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IO_READ_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes an IO read request.
TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IO_WRITE_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes an IO write request.
TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_I_STREAM_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes an I-stream request.
TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_MDF_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes an MDF request.
TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IREQ_IV_VALID  The event occurs when the L2 cache system processes a request with the opcode IREQ_IV.

TMC_PERF_UDN_PACKET_SENT  Main processor finished sending a packet to the UDN.
TMC_PERF_UDN_WORD_SENT  UDN word sent to an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the UDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_UDN_BUBBLE  Bubble detected on an output port.

A bubble is defined as a cycle in which no data is being sent, but the first word of a packet has already traversed the switch. Participating ports are selected with the UDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_UDN_CONGESTION  Out of credit on an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the UDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_IDN_PACKET_SENT  Main processor finished sending a packet to the IDN.
TMC_PERF_IDN_WORD_SENT  IDN word sent to an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the IDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_IDN_BUBBLE  Bubble detected on an output port.

A bubble is defined as a cycle in which no data is being sent, but the first word of a packet has already traversed the switch. Participating ports are selected with the IDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_IDN_CONGESTION  Out of credit on an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the IDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_RDN_PACKET_SENT  Main processor finished sending a packet to the RDN.
TMC_PERF_RDN_WORD_SENT  RDN word sent to an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the RDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_RDN_BUBBLE  Bubble detected on an output port.

A bubble is defined as a cycle in which no data is being sent, but the first word of a packet has already traversed the switch. Participating ports are selected with the RDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_RDN_CONGESTION  Out of credit on an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the RDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_SDN_PACKET_SENT  Main processor finished sending a packet to the SDN.
TMC_PERF_SDN_WORD_SENT  SDN word sent to an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the SDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_SDN_BUBBLE  Bubble detected on an output port.

A bubble is defined as a cycle in which no data is being sent, but the first word of a packet has already traversed the switch. Participating ports are selected with the SDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_SDN_CONGESTION  Out of credit on an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the SDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_QDN_PACKET_SENT  Main processor finished sending a packet to the QDN.
TMC_PERF_QDN_WORD_SENT  QDN word sent to an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the QDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_QDN_BUBBLE  Bubble detected on an output port.

A bubble is defined as a cycle in which no data is being sent, but the first word of a packet has already traversed the switch. Participating ports are selected with the QDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_QDN_CONGESTION  Out of credit on an output port.

Participating ports are selected with the QDN_EVT_PORT_SEL field.

TMC_PERF_UDN_DEMUX_STALL  UDN Demux stalled due to buffer full.
TMC_PERF_IDN_DEMUX_STALL  IDN Demux stalled due to buffer full.
TMC_PERF_WATCH  The event occurs when the Watch SPR detects a address or address range.
TMC_PERF_BCST0  The event occurs whenever broadcast wire-0 asserts.
TMC_PERF_BCST1  The event occurs whenever broadcast wire-1 asserts.
TMC_PERF_BCST2  The event occurs whenever broadcast wire-2 asserts.
TMC_PERF_BCST3  The event occurs whenever broadcast wire-3 asserts.
TMC_PERF_PCNT0  The event occurs when Performance counter-0 overflows.
TMC_PERF_PCNT1  The event occurs when Performance counter-1 overflows.
TMC_PERF_AUX_PCNT0  The event occurs when Auxiliary Performance counter-0 overflows.
TMC_PERF_AUX_PCNT1  The event occurs when Auxiliary Performance counter-1 overflows.

Function Documentation

int tmc_perf_enable_counters ( void   ) 

Enable accessing perf counters from user space.

Returns:
0 on success, or else -1 with errno set

int tmc_perf_disable_counters ( void   ) 

Disable accessing perf counters from user space.

Returns:
0 on success, or else -1 with errno set

int tmc_perf_setup_counters ( enum tmc_perf_events_e  event1,
enum tmc_perf_events_e  event2,
enum tmc_perf_events_e  event3,
enum tmc_perf_events_e  event4 
)

Select four events from the enum below to initialize the hardware counters.

Parameters:
event1 Used to set event for PERF_COUNT_1.
event2 Used to set event for PERF_COUNT_0.
event3 Used to set event for AUX_PERF_COUNT_1.
event4 Used to set event for AUX_PERF_COUNT_0.
Returns:
Zero if all of the events specified were valid, -1 otherwise; invalid events will be replaced by the ZERO event when the counters are configured.

void tmc_perf_read_counters ( struct tmc_perf_counters *  counters  ) 

Read the four hardware counters without clearing them.

Parameters:
counters Used to store the values of the four hardware counters.

void tmc_perf_clear_counters ( void   ) 

Clear the four hardware counters.

void tmc_perf_add_counters ( struct tmc_perf_counters *  counters  ) 

Accumulate counts from the four hardware counters without clearing them.

Parameters:
counters Used to store the values of the four hardware counters.

uint64_t tmc_perf_get_cpu_speed ( void   ) 

Get the CPU speed, in hertz.

Returns:
CPU speed.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

All other trademarks and/or registered trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).
